entities.name: TECHNOLOGY ADOPTION
entities.description: The process of farmers accepting and implementing improved agricultural technologies

entities.name: RESOURCEFUL FARMERS
entities.description: Farmers with greater access to resources who tend to adopt technologies more quickly

entities.name: ASSURED RAINFALL
entities.description: Stable precipitation patterns that facilitate faster technology adoption in core areas

entities.name: RESOURCE-POOR FARMERS
entities.description: Farmers with limited resources who experience a slower pace of technology adoption

entities.name: HINTERLANDS
entities.description: Less productive and diverse rural areas where technology adoption is slower

entities.name: INDIGENOUS KNOWLEDGE
entities.description: Local knowledge held by farmers, essential for developing and testing technology to suit local conditions

entities.name: FARMER-PARTICIPATORY ON-FARM RESEARCH
entities.description: An approach involving farmers in the development and testing of technology, serving as the foundation for adoption

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS
entities.description: External factors that influence technology adoption but are beyond human control

entities.name: INFRASTRUCTURE FOR ADOPTION
entities.description: The technological, institutional, socioeconomic, and human support systems that promote technology adoption

entities.name: BANGLADESH
entities.description: The country where studies were conducted to illustrate the benefits of infrastructural support systems

relationships.description: Associated with slower adoption due to diverse conditions
Used to develop and test technology to suit local conditions
Promotes and supports the adoption of improved technologies
Location where the benefits of support systems were illustrated
